text.title: ai-assisted-accessibility Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-assisted-accessibility as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using AI overlay widgets (UserWay, AccessiBe) as the sole compliance strategy
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Overlays do not fix underlying code issues. They are runtime patches that fail when JavaScript is disabled or with certain AT combinations. Legal challenges have succeeded against overlay-only compliance claims.

failure-mode.description: Agent runs axe-core without handling auth flows, cookie consent banners, or dynamic content loading
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: All three interfere with scan results. Agents must handle authentication, dismiss consent banners, and wait for dynamic content before running axe.

failure-mode.description: Agent maps violations to incorrect WCAG criteria (e.g., citing 1.4.3 Contrast for a keyboard navigation issue)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs confidently produce incorrect criterion references. Always validate criterion mappings against the WCAG 2.2 spec or a verified tool output before including in a report.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates generic, keyword-stuffed alt text for complex charts and infographics
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AI alt text defaults to describing the visual appearance, not the data or meaning. Prompt explicitly for functional, descriptive alt text that conveys the chart's insight.

failure-mode.description: Agent scans only the landing page of an SPA, missing component-level violations on navigated routes
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SPA frameworks rebuild the DOM on navigation. Agents must trigger navigation events and re-run axe on each route.

failure-mode.description: Agent marks an ARIA-dependent issue resolved without AT verification
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Assistive technology behavior (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver) diverges significantly from automated tool predictions. AT-dependent issues require AT verification before closing.
